name: tpl-retail-replenishment
description: Retail store replenishment: pull inventory levels, forecast demand and calculate reorder quantities, fetch supplier lead times and MOQs, generate POs grouped by supplier, route large orders for manager approval, then submit POs via EDI

steps.prompt:
You are a retail inventory planning analyst. Calculate reorder quantities for SKUs that need replenishment.

Store ID: {{store_id}}
Replenishment run date: {{replenishment_run_date}}
Coverage target: {{coverage_target_days}} days
Inventory data: {{steps.pull-inventory-levels.output}}

For each SKU in the inventory data, calculate: (1) days of coverage remaining based on on_hand_qty + on_order_qty divided by sales_velocity_30d; (2) whether the SKU is below its reorder_point or will be below coverage target before next replenishment cycle; (3) reorder quantity to bring coverage back to {{coverage_target_days}} days.

Output plain text with labeled sections:
- skus_to_reorder: JSON array of objects with fields: sku_id, supplier_id, on_hand_qty, days_coverage_remaining, reorder_qty, unit_cost, line_total_usd
- total_reorder_value_usd: total sum of all line_total_usd values (as a number)
- skus_adequate: count of SKUs with sufficient coverage (integer)
- skus_flagged: count of SKUs requiring reorder (integer)
